name: Conair InfinitiPRO 1875W AC Motor Salon Performance Hair Dryer

specs.Motor: 1875W AC motor (professional grade, longer lifespan than DC)
specs.Cold Shot: Cold shot button for setting style

whatWorks: The AC motor is the key differentiator in this price range. Consumer dryers almost always use DC motors that wear out in 1–3 years; the InfinitiPRO's AC motor runs significantly longer with consistent power output throughout its life. You're paying salon-tool durability at a consumer price.

At 1875W with ionic technology, this dryer is fast enough that most people with shoulder-length hair can finish in 10–15 minutes. The ions reduce frizz by neutralizing static charge during drying, which makes the difference between a result that looks blown out versus a result that looks air-dried with frizz.

The included diffuser and concentrator nozzle make this a genuinely versatile tool. The concentrator works for precision blowouts and straightening; the diffuser is essential for curly and wavy hair types who want to enhance their natural texture without disrupting the curl pattern.

A 9-foot cord in this price category is uncommon and practically useful — most budget dryers have 6-foot cords that limit mobility around the bathroom. This detail makes a real difference in daily use.
worthKnowing: At $49 CAD, this is not in the same class as the Dyson Supersonic or Shark HyperAir. The difference shows most in heat technology — it lacks the precision temperature control that prevents heat damage on fine hair. Use a heat protectant spray and don't hold it stationary on fine or color-treated hair.

The dryer is louder than premium models. If noise is a concern — early morning use, partners sleeping, apartment walls — consider a quieter model like the Dyson. The InfinitiPRO is about as loud as a typical salon dryer.

Ionic technology produces more negative ions on lower heat settings. Running the dryer at maximum heat while expecting maximum frizz control doesn't work — drop to medium heat to actually benefit from the ionic feature.

Attachments snap on securely but can feel plasticky compared to premium dryer attachments. The concentrator in particular is basic but functional — expect it to work perfectly well without feeling premium.

commonComplaints.title: Loud compared to my old dryer…
commonComplaints.body: I didn't expect a hair dryer to be quiet but this one is noticeably loud. My kids wake up if I use it before they're up. Functionality is great though.

commonComplaints.title: Gets very hot on max setting…
commonComplaints.body: The max heat setting is too hot for my fine hair. I stick to medium and it works fine, but first-time buyers should know not to crank it to max.

commonComplaints.title: Concentrator attachment pops off…
commonComplaints.body: The concentrator attachment doesn't clip on super firmly. It's never fallen off during use but if I bump it it comes right off.

commonComplaints.title: Heavy for long drying sessions…
commonComplaints.body: With thick long hair this takes 20+ minutes and my wrist gets tired. The dryer isn't unusually heavy but the length of use adds up.

verdict: The Conair InfinitiPRO 1875W is the best hair dryer under $60 CAD, full stop — the AC motor alone justifies the price difference over similarly priced DC motor dryers. It won't match the Dyson's precision heat technology or the Shark's smart features, but it dries hair fast, reduces frizz, includes both diffuser and concentrator, and will last for years. For most people who just need a reliable, capable daily dryer without premium pricing, this is the answer.

realBuyers.quote: I've been through three cheap dryers in four years. This is the first one with an AC motor and you can immediately tell the difference in power and consistency.
My hair dried in 12 minutes and looked smooth with almost no frizz. Can't believe this is $50 and performs this well.
The diffuser is the best part — it enhances my curls without frizzing them up like most dryers do. The ionic technology actually works.
Had my last Conair dryer for 6 years before the cord gave out. Bought another one. The AC motor is the real deal.

sources.title: Best Hair Dryers Under $100 — Good Housekeeping

metaDescription: Conair InfinitiPRO 1875W AC motor hair dryer review for Canada. Best budget dryer pick with ionic tech, diffuser, concentrator. ~$49 CAD on Amazon.ca.
introText: The Conair InfinitiPRO 1875W uses a professional-grade AC motor for longer life and consistent power, with ionic technology and both a diffuser and concentrator nozzle included. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B07GQ8RTWB) at around $49 CAD.
